Petition For Settlement Of First Account And Report Of Trustee And For Allowance Of Attorneys And Trustees Fees
Off Calendar May-10-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Roosevelt for and with Derek Knudsen.

Mr. Roosevelt confirmed that the $10,850.00 amount is part of the $29,350.00 fee request. The Court granted the Petition to Approve First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition To Compel Trustee To Account; To Remove Trustee And To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Continued To Sep-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jeanne DeJong (via court call) for (petitioner) Mildred Liles. Michael Roosevelt for (trustee) Derek Knudsen.

The Court asked Mr. Roosevelt if they could get someone else in place such as Mr. Weiss. Mr. Roosevelt reported that accountings in both trusts - the Derby trust and Don Liles Trust are nearly complete, that there are a few loose ends to tie up, that they believe the accountings which go through 6/30/16 can be completed within 30 days, that they would like a status date set for that time frame, that Mr. Knudsen has taken this very seriously, that he has been doing this for over 40 years and is a well respected lawyer, that there is an end in sight for these trusts which are expected to be fully distributed by the end of the year and that it would take more time to bring in a new person and get them up to speed. Ms. DeJong stated that they have no objection to the status date, that they reaffirm their request that Mr. Knudsen be removed, that they have had nothing to go on, that they want a professional fiduciary in place and that due to Mr. Knudsen's health issues they want him replaced. Mr. Roosevelt reported that there was a beginning balance of $3,722,000.00 as of the date of death, that all assets have been distributed, that there is cash in the amount of $130,000.00 to cover the accountings, trustee and attorneys fees, that they are down to 2-3% of the original assets and that a new trustee could not get this done by the end of the year. Ms. DeJong stated she has nothing further. The Court stated that it will not remove Mr. Knudsen today, set a status on filing the accounting for September 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and stated that if the accountings are straightforward it is likely they can get to the finish without removal and that the removal can be revisited at that time. Ms. DeJong stated that they request that Mr. Knudsen be charged with fees and costs associated with this filing. The Court stated that it is noted for the record. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account; to Remove Trustee and to Appoint a Successor Trustee was continued to September 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
